title: [API] health: GET /api/health returns 200 with ok status and mongo state
preconditions: Backend server is running and accessible
MongoDB connection is established

steps.action: Send GET /api/health with header Accept: application/json
steps.expectedResult: Response status code is 200

steps.action: Inspect the response Content-Type header
steps.expectedResult: Content-Type header contains application/json

steps.action: Parse the response JSON body and verify the 'status' field exists
steps.expectedResult: Response body contains a 'status' field with value 'ok'

steps.action: Verify the response JSON body contains a field indicating MongoDB connection state
steps.expectedResult: Response body contains a mongo/database state field (e.g., 'mongo' or 'db') with a value indicating connected/ready state

expectedOutcome: GET /api/health returns HTTP 200 with JSON containing status 'ok' and a healthy mongo connection state

title: [API] health: POST method to /api/health is rejected
preconditions: Backend server is running and accessible

steps.action: Send POST /api/health with empty JSON body {} and header Content-Type: application/json
steps.expectedResult: Response status code is 404 or 405 (Method Not Allowed)

steps.action: Verify the response does not return the normal health status payload
steps.expectedResult: Response body does not contain 'status': 'ok'

expectedOutcome: Health endpoint rejects POST requests with a 404 or 405 status code

title: [API] health: Request with extra query parameters still returns 200
preconditions: Backend server is running and accessible

steps.action: Send GET /api/health?foo=bar&baz=123
steps.expectedResult: Response status code is 200

steps.action: Parse response JSON and verify 'status' field
steps.expectedResult: Response body contains 'status': 'ok'; extra query params are ignored gracefully

expectedOutcome: Health endpoint ignores unexpected query parameters and returns normal 200 response

title: [API] employees: List employees with default pagination returns data array and pagination metadata
preconditions: At least 1 employee exists in the database
API server is running

steps.action: Send GET /api/employees with no query parameters
steps.expectedResult: Response status is 200

steps.action: Inspect the response Content-Type header
steps.expectedResult: Content-Type is application/json

steps.action: Inspect the response body for a 'data' field
steps.expectedResult: 'data' field exists and is an array

steps.action: Inspect each element in the 'data' array for required fields (id, firstName, lastName, email, department)
steps.expectedResult: Each employee object contains id, firstName, lastName, email, and department fields with non-null values

steps.action: Inspect the response body for pagination metadata fields (page, limit, total, totalPages)
steps.expectedResult: Response body contains 'page', 'limit', 'total', and 'totalPages' fields, all as numbers

steps.action: Verify default pagination values: page and limit
steps.expectedResult: 'page' is 1 and 'limit' is a positive integer (e.g., 10 or 20 — the server default)

steps.action: Verify that the length of 'data' array is less than or equal to the 'limit' value
steps.expectedResult: data.length <= limit

expectedOutcome: GET /api/employees with no params returns 200 with a data array and valid pagination metadata using server defaults

title: [API] employees: Request page beyond available data returns empty array
preconditions: At least 1 employee exists in the database
API server is running

steps.action: Send GET /api/employees?page=1&limit=10 to determine total employee count
steps.expectedResult: Response status is 200 and 'total' field is returned

steps.action: Calculate a page number that exceeds totalPages (e.g., totalPages + 10)
steps.expectedResult: Page number is computed

steps.action: Send GET /api/employees?page={computed_page}&limit=10
steps.expectedResult: Response status is 200

steps.action: Inspect the 'data' field in the response body
steps.expectedResult: 'data' is an empty array []

steps.action: Inspect pagination metadata
steps.expectedResult: 'total' and 'totalPages' remain consistent with actual data count; 'page' reflects the requested page number

expectedOutcome: Requesting a page beyond the last page returns an empty data array with valid pagination metadata

title: [API] employees: Unsupported HTTP method returns 404 or 405
preconditions: API server is running

steps.action: Send DELETE /api/employees (without specifying an employee ID)
steps.expectedResult: Response status is 404 or 405 Method Not Allowed

steps.action: Send PATCH /api/employees with empty body
steps.expectedResult: Response status is 404 or 405 Method Not Allowed

steps.action: Verify response body contains an error message or is not a valid employee listing
steps.expectedResult: Response does not return a 'data' array of employees

expectedOutcome: Non-GET methods on the /api/employees collection endpoint are rejected appropriately

title: [API] employees: Filter by department and status returns matching results
preconditions: At least one employee exists with department=QA and status=Active
At least one employee exists with department=Engineering and status=Active
At least one employee exists with department=QA and status=Inactive

steps.action: Send GET /api/employees?department=QA&status=Active
steps.expectedResult: Response status 200 with Content-Type application/json

steps.action: Parse the response JSON array and inspect each element's 'department' field
steps.expectedResult: Every returned employee object has department equal to 'QA'

steps.action: Inspect each element's 'status' field in the response JSON array
steps.expectedResult: Every returned employee object has status equal to 'Active'

steps.action: Verify the response array length is greater than 0
steps.expectedResult: At least one employee is returned matching both filters

expectedOutcome: Only employees with department=QA AND status=Active are returned; no employees from other departments or with other statuses appear

title: [API] employees: Invalid status value returns empty array or 400
preconditions: Employees exist in the system

steps.action: Send GET /api/employees?department=QA&status=InvalidStatus
steps.expectedResult: Response status is either 200 with an empty array or 400 with an error message indicating invalid status value

steps.action: If response is 200, parse the JSON array
steps.expectedResult: The array is empty (no employees match an invalid status)

steps.action: If response is 400, parse the response body for error details
steps.expectedResult: Response body contains an error message referencing invalid status parameter

expectedOutcome: The API gracefully handles an invalid status value by returning no results or a descriptive validation error

title: [API] employees: Search by partial first name returns matching records
preconditions: At least one employee with first name containing 'Thava' exists in the database
The API server is running and accessible

steps.action: Send GET /api/employees?search=Thava
steps.expectedResult: Response status is 200 and Content-Type is application/json

steps.action: Parse the response JSON body and inspect the returned array of employee records
steps.expectedResult: Response body is a JSON array (or contains a data array) with at least 1 employee record

steps.action: Verify each returned employee record contains 'Thava' (case-insensitive) in at least one of: firstName, lastName, name, email, or designation fields
steps.expectedResult: Every record in the result set matches the search term 'Thava' in at least one searchable field

expectedOutcome: GET /api/employees?search=Thava returns HTTP 200 with one or more employee records matching 'Thava' in name, email, or designation

title: [API] employees: Search with no matching term returns empty result set
preconditions: No employee exists with 'ZZXXYY99nonexistent' in name, email, or designation
The API server is running and accessible

steps.action: Send GET /api/employees?search=ZZXXYY99nonexistent
steps.expectedResult: Response status is 200 and Content-Type is application/json

steps.action: Parse the response JSON body
steps.expectedResult: Response body is an empty array [] or contains a data array with length 0

steps.action: Verify no employee records are returned in the result
steps.expectedResult: Result count is 0

expectedOutcome: Searching for a non-existent term returns HTTP 200 with an empty result set and no errors

title: [API] employees: Search with empty string returns all employees
preconditions: Multiple employee records exist in the database
The API server is running and accessible

steps.action: Send GET /api/employees to get total employee count (baseline)
steps.expectedResult: Response status is 200 and returns all employees

steps.action: Record the total count of employees from step 1 response
steps.expectedResult: Total count is captured for comparison

steps.action: Send GET /api/employees?search=
steps.expectedResult: Response status is 200 and Content-Type is application/json

steps.action: Compare the result count from step 3 with the baseline count from step 2
steps.expectedResult: Both counts are equal — empty search returns all employees (unfiltered)

expectedOutcome: An empty search parameter returns the full list of employees, equivalent to no filter applied

title: [API] employees: Create employee with all required valid fields returns 201
preconditions: API server is running
Database is accessible and empty or in known state

steps.action: Send POST /api/employees with JSON body: {"firstName":"John","lastName":"Doe","email":"john.doe@example.com","department":"Engineering","position":"Software Engineer"} and header Content-Type: application/json
steps.expectedResult: Response status code is 201

steps.action: Parse the response JSON body
steps.expectedResult: Response Content-Type is application/json. Body contains fields: id (non-null), firstName ("John"), lastName ("Doe"), email ("john.doe@example.com"), department ("Engineering"), position ("Software Engineer")

steps.action: Send GET /api/employees/{id} using the id returned in step 2
steps.expectedResult: Response status code is 200 and returned employee matches the data submitted in step 1

expectedOutcome: Employee is successfully created, persisted, and retrievable with all submitted field values intact

title: [API] employees: Create employee with missing required firstName returns 400/422
preconditions: API server is running

steps.action: Send POST /api/employees with JSON body: {"lastName":"Doe","email":"missing.first@example.com","department":"Engineering"} and header Content-Type: application/json (firstName omitted)
steps.expectedResult: Response status code is 400 or 422

steps.action: Parse the response JSON body
steps.expectedResult: Response body contains an error message indicating firstName is required

steps.action: Send GET /api/employees and search for email "missing.first@example.com" in the results
steps.expectedResult: No employee with that email exists, confirming no record was created

expectedOutcome: Server rejects the request with a validation error and no employee record is persisted

title: [API] employees: Create employee with boundary-length field values
preconditions: API server is running

steps.action: Send POST /api/employees with JSON body: {"firstName":"A","lastName":"B","email":"a@b.co","department":"D"} and header Content-Type: application/json (minimum-length values)
steps.expectedResult: Response status code is 201 or a 400/422 if minimum length constraints exist, but not 500

steps.action: Generate a 256-character string for firstName. Send POST /api/employees with JSON body: {"firstName":"<256-char-string>","lastName":"Long","email":"longname@example.com","department":"Engineering"} and header Content-Type: application/json
steps.expectedResult: Response status code is either 201 (if accepted) or 400/422 (if length validation is enforced), not 500

steps.action: Send POST /api/employees with JSON body: {"firstName":"","lastName":"","email":"","department":""} and header Content-Type: application/json (empty string values)
steps.expectedResult: Response status code is 400 or 422 with validation errors for empty required fields

expectedOutcome: Server handles boundary-length inputs gracefully with proper validation and no 500 errors

title: [API] employees: POST with empty body returns 400 with validation details
preconditions: API server is running and accessible

steps.action: Send POST /api/employees with header Content-Type: application/json and empty JSON body: {}
steps.expectedResult: Response status is 400

steps.action: Parse the response JSON body
steps.expectedResult: Response Content-Type is application/json

steps.action: Inspect the response body for validation error details
steps.expectedResult: Response body contains validation errors referencing required fields (e.g., firstName, email, department or similar required fields)

expectedOutcome: Server rejects the request with HTTP 400 and returns a JSON body containing specific validation error messages for all missing required fields

title: [API] employees: POST with all valid required fields returns 201 (happy path)
preconditions: API server is running and accessible

steps.action: Send POST /api/employees with header Content-Type: application/json and body: {"firstName":"Jane","lastName":"Doe","email":"jane.doe@example.com","department":"Engineering"}
steps.expectedResult: Response status is 201

steps.action: Parse the response JSON body
steps.expectedResult: Response Content-Type is application/json

steps.action: Verify the response body contains an id field
steps.expectedResult: Response body field 'id' exists and is a non-null value

steps.action: Verify the response body echoes back firstName, lastName, email, and department
steps.expectedResult: firstName is 'Jane', lastName is 'Doe', email is 'jane.doe@example.com', department is 'Engineering'

expectedOutcome: Employee is successfully created and response includes the new employee record with an assigned id

title: [API] employees: POST with multiple missing required fields returns 400 listing all errors
preconditions: API server is running and accessible

steps.action: Send POST /api/employees with header Content-Type: application/json and body containing only lastName: {"lastName":"Doe"}
steps.expectedResult: Response status is 400

steps.action: Parse the response JSON body
steps.expectedResult: Response Content-Type is application/json

steps.action: Count the number of distinct validation errors in the response body
steps.expectedResult: Response body contains validation errors for at least firstName, email, and department (multiple errors returned, not just the first one)

steps.action: Verify each error references the specific missing field name
steps.expectedResult: Each validation error message clearly identifies which field is missing or invalid

expectedOutcome: Server returns all validation errors at once rather than failing on only the first missing field, enabling clients to fix all issues in a single pass

title: [API] employees: Duplicate email returns 409 conflict on second creation
preconditions: API server is running
Database is accessible and clean or in a known state

steps.action: Send POST /api/employees with JSON body: {"firstName":"Alice","lastName":"Smith","email":"alice.dup@example.com","department":"Engineering"}
steps.expectedResult: Response 201 with JSON body containing id, firstName, lastName, email fields

steps.action: Capture the id from the step 1 response for cleanup later
steps.expectedResult: id is a valid non-null value

steps.action: Send POST /api/employees with JSON body using the same email: {"firstName":"Bob","lastName":"Jones","email":"alice.dup@example.com","department":"Marketing"}
steps.expectedResult: Response 409 with JSON body containing error code DUPLICATE_EMAIL or message indicating email already exists

steps.action: Verify the response Content-Type header is application/json
steps.expectedResult: Content-Type includes application/json

steps.action: Verify the 409 response body contains an error field or message referencing DUPLICATE_EMAIL
steps.expectedResult: Response JSON includes error code DUPLICATE_EMAIL or descriptive duplicate email message

steps.action: Send DELETE /api/employees/{id} using the id captured in step 2 to clean up
steps.expectedResult: Response 200 or 204 confirming deletion

expectedOutcome: Second POST with the same email is rejected with 409 and DUPLICATE_EMAIL error; no duplicate record is created

title: [API] employees: First employee creation with unique email succeeds (happy path)
preconditions: API server is running
No employee with email unique.happy@example.com exists

steps.action: Send POST /api/employees with JSON body: {"firstName":"Happy","lastName":"Path","email":"unique.happy@example.com","department":"Sales"}
steps.expectedResult: Response 201 with JSON body containing id, firstName, lastName, email, department

steps.action: Verify response JSON field email equals "unique.happy@example.com"
steps.expectedResult: email field is "unique.happy@example.com"

steps.action: Verify response Content-Type header is application/json
steps.expectedResult: Content-Type includes application/json

steps.action: Send DELETE /api/employees/{id} using the id from step 1 to clean up
steps.expectedResult: Response 200 or 204 confirming deletion

expectedOutcome: Employee is created successfully with 201 and all provided fields are returned correctly

title: [API] employees: Duplicate email check is case-insensitive
preconditions: API server is running
No employee with email casetest@example.com exists

steps.action: Send POST /api/employees with JSON body: {"firstName":"Case","lastName":"Lower","email":"casetest@example.com","department":"Engineering"}
steps.expectedResult: Response 201 with JSON body containing the created employee

steps.action: Capture the id from step 1 response
steps.expectedResult: id is a valid non-null value

steps.action: Send POST /api/employees with JSON body using uppercase variant: {"firstName":"Case","lastName":"Upper","email":"CASETEST@EXAMPLE.COM","department":"Marketing"}
steps.expectedResult: Response 409 with DUPLICATE_EMAIL error indicating the email already exists

steps.action: Send POST /api/employees with JSON body using mixed case: {"firstName":"Case","lastName":"Mixed","email":"CaseTest@Example.COM","department":"HR"}
steps.expectedResult: Response 409 with DUPLICATE_EMAIL error

steps.action: Send DELETE /api/employees/{id} using the id from step 2 to clean up
steps.expectedResult: Response 200 or 204 confirming deletion

expectedOutcome: Duplicate email detection is case-insensitive; uppercase and mixed-case variants of an existing email are rejected with 409

title: [API] employees: GET /api/employees/:id returns full employee object for valid ID
preconditions: At least one employee exists in the database
The employee ID used in the test is known and valid

steps.action: Send POST /api/employees with JSON body: {"firstName":"Alice","lastName":"Smith","email":"alice.smith@example.com","department":"Engineering","position":"Developer"}
steps.expectedResult: Response 201 with JSON body containing an 'id' field and the submitted employee data

steps.action: Extract the 'id' value from the step 1 response body
steps.expectedResult: A valid employee ID is captured for subsequent requests

steps.action: Send GET /api/employees/:id using the extracted ID
steps.expectedResult: Response status is 200

steps.action: Verify the response Content-Type header is 'application/json'
steps.expectedResult: Content-Type header contains 'application/json'

steps.action: Verify the response body contains fields: id, firstName, lastName, email, department, position
steps.expectedResult: All fields are present in the response JSON object

steps.action: Verify response body field values match: firstName='Alice', lastName='Smith', email='alice.smith@example.com', department='Engineering', position='Developer'
steps.expectedResult: All field values match the originally created employee data

expectedOutcome: GET /api/employees/:id returns 200 with a complete and accurate employee JSON object matching the created record

title: [API] employees: GET /api/employees/:id returns 404 for non-existent ID
preconditions: No employee exists with the ID used in the test (e.g., 999999)

steps.action: Send GET /api/employees/999999
steps.expectedResult: Response status is 404

steps.action: Verify the response Content-Type header is 'application/json'
steps.expectedResult: Content-Type header contains 'application/json'

steps.action: Verify the response body contains an error message indicating the employee was not found
steps.expectedResult: Response JSON contains a message field such as 'Employee not found' or similar error text

expectedOutcome: GET /api/employees/:id with a non-existent ID returns 404 with an appropriate error message

title: [API] employees: GET /api/employees/:id returns 400 or 404 for invalid non-numeric ID
preconditions: Application is running and /api/employees endpoint is accessible

steps.action: Send GET /api/employees/abc
steps.expectedResult: Response status is 400 or 404 (application rejects the malformed ID)

steps.action: Verify the response body contains an error message indicating invalid ID or employee not found
steps.expectedResult: Response JSON includes a descriptive error message

steps.action: Send GET /api/employees/!@%23%24
steps.expectedResult: Response status is 400 or 404

steps.action: Verify the response body contains an error message
steps.expectedResult: Response JSON includes a descriptive error message

expectedOutcome: GET /api/employees/:id with non-numeric or special-character IDs returns 400 or 404 with a meaningful error

title: [API] employees: Malformed string ID returns 400 INVALID_ID error
preconditions: API server is running and accessible

steps.action: Send GET /api/employees/invalid-id with header Accept: application/json
steps.expectedResult: Response status code is 400

steps.action: Inspect the response Content-Type header
steps.expectedResult: Content-Type is application/json

steps.action: Parse the response JSON body and check for error code field
steps.expectedResult: Response body contains error code 'INVALID_ID' or equivalent error message indicating the ID is malformed

expectedOutcome: GET /api/employees/invalid-id returns HTTP 400 with INVALID_ID error code in JSON response

title: [API] employees: Special characters in ID returns 400 INVALID_ID
preconditions: API server is running and accessible

steps.action: Send GET /api/employees/!@%23$%25 with header Accept: application/json
steps.expectedResult: Response status code is 400

steps.action: Parse the response JSON body
steps.expectedResult: Response body contains error code 'INVALID_ID'

steps.action: Verify the response Content-Type header
steps.expectedResult: Content-Type is application/json

expectedOutcome: Special characters in the employee ID parameter trigger a 400 INVALID_ID response

title: [API] employees: PATCH updates a single field and returns updated employee
preconditions: At least one employee exists in the database

steps.action: Send POST /api/employees with JSON body: {"firstName":"Alice","lastName":"Smith","email":"alice.smith@example.com","department":"Engineering","position":"Developer"}
steps.expectedResult: Response 201 with JSON body containing an 'id' field and the submitted data

steps.action: Capture the 'id' from the response body of step 1
steps.expectedResult: A valid employee ID is stored for subsequent steps

steps.action: Send PATCH /api/employees/:id with JSON body: {"firstName":"Alicia"} using the captured ID
steps.expectedResult: Response 200 with JSON body where firstName is 'Alicia' and all other fields (lastName, email, department, position) remain unchanged

steps.action: Send GET /api/employees/:id using the captured ID
steps.expectedResult: Response 200 with JSON body confirming firstName is 'Alicia', lastName is 'Smith', email is 'alice.smith@example.com', department is 'Engineering'

expectedOutcome: PATCH successfully updates only the specified field and persists the change; unmodified fields remain intact

title: [API] employees: PATCH with non-existent employee ID returns 404
preconditions: No employee exists with the ID used in the test

steps.action: Send PATCH /api/employees/999999 with JSON body: {"firstName":"Ghost"} and header Content-Type: application/json
steps.expectedResult: Response 404 with JSON body containing an error message indicating the employee was not found

expectedOutcome: PATCH to a non-existent employee ID returns HTTP 404 with an appropriate error message

title: [API] employees: PATCH with empty request body returns error or no-op
preconditions: At least one employee exists in the database

steps.action: Send POST /api/employees with JSON body: {"firstName":"Carol","lastName":"Davis","email":"carol.davis@example.com","department":"HR","position":"Analyst"}
steps.expectedResult: Response 201 with JSON body containing an 'id' field

steps.action: Capture the 'id' from the response body of step 1
steps.expectedResult: A valid employee ID is stored

steps.action: Send PATCH /api/employees/:id with JSON body: {} and header Content-Type: application/json
steps.expectedResult: Response is either 200 with unchanged employee data, or 400 indicating no fields to update

steps.action: Send GET /api/employees/:id using the captured ID
steps.expectedResult: Response 200 with all original fields unchanged: firstName 'Carol', lastName 'Davis', email 'carol.davis@example.com'

expectedOutcome: Sending an empty PATCH body does not corrupt data; employee remains unchanged

title: [API] employees: PATCH with invalid email format returns validation error
preconditions: At least one employee exists in the database

steps.action: Send POST /api/employees with JSON body: {"firstName":"Eve","lastName":"Brown","email":"eve.brown@example.com","department":"IT","position":"Support"}
steps.expectedResult: Response 201 with JSON body containing an 'id' field

steps.action: Capture the 'id' from the response body of step 1
steps.expectedResult: A valid employee ID is stored

steps.action: Send PATCH /api/employees/:id with JSON body: {"email":"not-an-email"} using the captured ID
steps.expectedResult: Response 400 or 422 with JSON body containing a validation error message about invalid email format

steps.action: Send GET /api/employees/:id using the captured ID
steps.expectedResult: Response 200 with email still 'eve.brown@example.com' — the invalid update was rejected

expectedOutcome: PATCH rejects an invalid email format and does not modify the employee record

title: [API] employees: DELETE valid employee returns 204 and removes record
preconditions: API server is running
Database is accessible

steps.action: Send POST /api/employees with JSON body: {"firstName":"John","lastName":"Doe","email":"john.doe.del@test.com","department":"Engineering"}
steps.expectedResult: Response 201 with JSON body containing an 'id' field

steps.action: Capture the 'id' from the step 1 response body
steps.expectedResult: A valid employee ID is stored for subsequent steps

steps.action: Send GET /api/employees/:id using the captured ID
steps.expectedResult: Response 200 with JSON body containing the created employee data

steps.action: Send DELETE /api/employees/:id using the captured ID
steps.expectedResult: Response status code is 204 with no response body (or empty body)

steps.action: Send GET /api/employees/:id using the same captured ID
steps.expectedResult: Response status code is 404 indicating the employee no longer exists

expectedOutcome: DELETE request for a valid employee ID returns 204 and the employee is no longer retrievable

title: [API] routing: GET /api/nonexistent returns 404 NOT_FOUND
preconditions: Backend server is running and accessible

steps.action: Send GET /api/nonexistent with header Accept: application/json
steps.expectedResult: Response status code is 404

steps.action: Inspect the response Content-Type header
steps.expectedResult: Content-Type includes application/json

steps.action: Parse the response JSON body and check for an error indicator field (e.g., 'message', 'error', or 'status')
steps.expectedResult: Response JSON contains a field indicating NOT_FOUND or 404 status, such as message containing 'not found' (case-insensitive) or status equal to 404

expectedOutcome: A request to an unknown API route returns HTTP 404 with a JSON error body indicating the route was not found

title: [API] routing: POST /api/nonexistent returns 404 NOT_FOUND
preconditions: Backend server is running and accessible

steps.action: Send POST /api/nonexistent with header Content-Type: application/json and body {"key":"value"}

steps.action: Inspect the response Content-Type header
steps.expectedResult: Content-Type includes application/json

steps.action: Parse the response JSON body
steps.expectedResult: Response JSON contains an error/message field indicating the route was not found

expectedOutcome: A POST request to an unknown API route returns HTTP 404 with a JSON error body

title: [API] routing: PUT /api/nonexistent returns 404 NOT_FOUND
preconditions: Backend server is running and accessible

steps.expectedResult: Response status code is 404

steps.action: Parse the response JSON body
steps.expectedResult: Response JSON contains an error indicator consistent with NOT_FOUND

expectedOutcome: A PUT request to an unknown API route returns HTTP 404 with a JSON error response

title: [API] routing: DELETE /api/nonexistent returns 404 NOT_FOUND
preconditions: Backend server is running and accessible

steps.action: Send DELETE /api/nonexistent with header Accept: application/json
steps.expectedResult: Response status code is 404

steps.action: Parse the response JSON body
steps.expectedResult: Response JSON contains an error indicator consistent with NOT_FOUND

expectedOutcome: A DELETE request to an unknown API route returns HTTP 404 with a JSON error response

title: [API] routing: PATCH /api/nonexistent returns 404 NOT_FOUND
preconditions: Backend server is running and accessible

steps.expectedResult: Response status code is 404

steps.action: Parse the response JSON body
steps.expectedResult: Response JSON contains an error indicator consistent with NOT_FOUND

title: [API] routing: Deeply nested unknown path returns 404
preconditions: Backend server is running and accessible

steps.action: Send GET /api/this/path/does/not/exist/at/all with header Accept: application/json
steps.expectedResult: Response status code is 404

steps.action: Inspect the response Content-Type header
steps.expectedResult: Content-Type includes application/json

steps.action: Parse the response JSON body
steps.expectedResult: Response JSON contains an error indicator consistent with NOT_FOUND

expectedOutcome: A deeply nested unknown API path returns HTTP 404 with a proper JSON error body, not a server error

title: [API] routing: Unknown path with query parameters returns 404
preconditions: Backend server is running and accessible

steps.action: Send GET /api/nonexistent?foo=bar&baz=123 with header Accept: application/json
steps.expectedResult: Response status code is 404

steps.action: Parse the response JSON body
steps.expectedResult: Response JSON contains an error indicator consistent with NOT_FOUND; query parameters do not cause a 500 or different error

expectedOutcome: Query parameters on an unknown route do not change the 404 behaviour

title: [API] routing: Unknown path with special characters returns 404 and not 500
preconditions: Backend server is running and accessible

steps.action: Send GET /api/non%20existent%2F%3Cscript%3E with header Accept: application/json
steps.expectedResult: Response status code is 404 (not 500 or other server error)

steps.action: Inspect the response Content-Type header
steps.expectedResult: Content-Type includes application/json

steps.action: Parse the response body
steps.expectedResult: Response body does not contain a stack trace or internal server error details; it returns a standard 404 JSON error

expectedOutcome: Special/encoded characters in an unknown path are handled gracefully with a 404 response, not a server crash or 500

title: [API] routing: Unknown route outside /api prefix returns 404
preconditions: Backend server is running and accessible

steps.action: Send GET /completely/random/path with header Accept: application/json
steps.expectedResult: Response status code is 404

steps.action: Verify the response does not return a 500 status code
steps.expectedResult: Status code is 404, not 500 or any other server error

expectedOutcome: Requests to unknown paths outside the /api prefix are also handled as 404 rather than causing server errors

title: [API] routing: Unknown route with request body on GET still returns 404
preconditions: Backend server is running and accessible

steps.action: Send GET /api/nonexistent with headers Content-Type: application/json and Accept: application/json, and body {"unexpected":"payload"}
steps.expectedResult: Response status code is 404

steps.action: Parse the response JSON body
steps.expectedResult: Response JSON contains a NOT_FOUND error indicator; the unexpected body does not cause a 400 or 500

expectedOutcome: Sending a body with a GET request to an unknown route still returns 404 without server errors

title: [UI] employees-page: Page loads and displays employee table with data rows
preconditions: At least one employee record exists in the system
The application is running and accessible at the root URL

steps.action: Open a browser and navigate to the root URL '/'
steps.expectedResult: The EmployeesPage loads without errors and the page is fully rendered

steps.action: Observe the top section of the page for a topbar or header area
steps.expectedResult: A topbar is visible containing page title, controls, or navigation elements related to the employees module

steps.action: Locate the employee table on the page
steps.expectedResult: An HTML table (or table-like component) is visible on the page with column headers such as Name, Email, Department, or similar employee attributes

steps.action: Inspect the table body for data rows
steps.expectedResult: At least one data row is rendered inside the table body, each row displaying employee information in the appropriate columns

steps.action: Verify that the data in the first row contains non-empty text in each visible column
steps.expectedResult: Every cell in the first data row contains visible, non-empty text corresponding to employee attributes

expectedOutcome: The employees page loads at root, renders a topbar and a fully populated employee table with at least one data row

title: [UI] employees-page: Empty state when no employees exist
preconditions: The system has zero employee records (database is empty or all employees are deleted)
The application is running and accessible at the root URL

steps.action: Navigate to '/' in the browser
steps.expectedResult: The EmployeesPage loads without crashing or showing a white screen

steps.action: Observe the topbar area of the page
steps.expectedResult: The topbar is still rendered and visible even when no data exists

steps.action: Locate the employee table or the area where the table should be
steps.expectedResult: Either the table is rendered with zero data rows (empty tbody) or an empty-state message is displayed (e.g., 'No employees found')

steps.action: Verify no JavaScript errors appear in the browser console
steps.expectedResult: The browser console shows no unhandled errors or exceptions related to rendering

expectedOutcome: When no employees exist, the page renders gracefully with either an empty table or an informative empty-state message, without crashes

title: [UI] employees-page: Search by full employee name filters list to matching result
preconditions: At least 5 employees exist in the system with distinct names (e.g., 'Alice Johnson', 'Bob Smith', 'Carlos Diaz', 'Diana Prince', 'Eve Torres')
The employees page is accessible at its designated route

steps.action: Navigate to the employees page (e.g., /employees)
steps.expectedResult: The employees page loads and a table/list of all employees is displayed with at least 5 rows

steps.action: Locate the search input field on the page
steps.expectedResult: A search input field is visible, enabled, and has placeholder text (e.g., 'Search employees…' or similar)

steps.action: Click into the search input and type 'Alice Johnson'
steps.expectedResult: The text 'Alice Johnson' appears in the search input field

steps.action: Wait for the employee list/table to update (debounce or immediate)
steps.expectedResult: The employee table filters down and shows only rows containing 'Alice Johnson'

steps.action: Count the number of rows displayed in the employee table
steps.expectedResult: Exactly 1 employee row is displayed showing 'Alice Johnson'

steps.action: Verify that the other employees (Bob Smith, Carlos Diaz, Diana Prince, Eve Torres) are NOT visible in the table
steps.expectedResult: No rows for Bob Smith, Carlos Diaz, Diana Prince, or Eve Torres are shown

expectedOutcome: Typing a full employee name in the search input filters the employee list to show only the matching employee

title: [UI] employees-page: Search with no matching results shows empty state
preconditions: At least 3 employees exist in the system
No employee has the name or attribute matching 'ZZZZNONEXISTENT'

steps.action: Navigate to the employees page (e.g., /employees)
steps.expectedResult: The employees page loads with all employees visible in the table

steps.action: Click into the search input and type 'ZZZZNONEXISTENT'
steps.expectedResult: The text 'ZZZZNONEXISTENT' appears in the search input

steps.action: Wait for the employee list to update
steps.expectedResult: The employee table shows zero rows

steps.action: Check for an empty state message or indicator
steps.expectedResult: An empty state message is displayed (e.g., 'No employees found', 'No results', or similar text indicating no matches)

steps.action: Verify that no employee data rows are rendered in the table body
steps.expectedResult: The table body contains no employee rows; only the empty state message is shown

expectedOutcome: Searching for a non-existent term results in an empty table with an appropriate empty state message

title: [UI] employees-page: Single character search filters list accordingly
preconditions: Multiple employees exist in the system, some with names starting with or containing the letter 'A'

steps.action: Navigate to the employees page (e.g., /employees)
steps.expectedResult: The employees page loads and all employees are displayed

steps.action: Note the total number of employee rows displayed
steps.expectedResult: The full list of employees is visible with a known count

steps.action: Click into the search input and type a single character 'A'
steps.expectedResult: The character 'A' appears in the search input

steps.action: Wait for the employee list to update
steps.expectedResult: The table filters to show only employees whose searchable fields contain the letter 'A' (case-insensitive)

steps.action: Verify that every displayed row contains the letter 'a' or 'A' somewhere in the visible employee data
steps.expectedResult: All displayed rows contain the character 'a'/'A' in the name or other searchable field

expectedOutcome: Even a single character triggers filtering and only matching employees are shown

title: [UI] employees-page: Selecting a department filter updates the employee list to show only matching employees
preconditions: At least 3 employees exist across different departments (e.g., Engineering, Marketing, Sales)
At least 1 employee belongs to the Engineering department

steps.action: Navigate to the employees page at /employees
steps.expectedResult: The employees page loads and displays the full list of employees with department and status filter dropdowns visible

steps.action: Note the total number of employees displayed in the list
steps.expectedResult: The full unfiltered list is rendered with all employees visible

steps.action: Click the department filter dropdown
steps.expectedResult: A dropdown menu opens showing all available department options (e.g., Engineering, Marketing, Sales)

steps.action: Select 'Engineering' from the department filter dropdown
steps.expectedResult: The dropdown closes and shows 'Engineering' as the selected value

steps.action: Observe the employee list after the filter is applied
steps.expectedResult: The employee list refreshes and displays only employees belonging to the Engineering department; employees from other departments are no longer visible

expectedOutcome: The employee list is correctly filtered to show only Engineering department employees when the department filter is set to Engineering

title: [UI] employees-page: Combining department and status filters narrows the employee list correctly
preconditions: At least 1 employee exists in Engineering with Active status
At least 1 employee exists in Engineering with Inactive status
At least 1 employee exists in Marketing with Active status

steps.action: Navigate to /employees
steps.expectedResult: The employees page loads with the full unfiltered list

steps.action: Click the department filter dropdown and select 'Engineering'
steps.expectedResult: The list updates to show only Engineering department employees (both Active and Inactive)

steps.action: Click the status filter dropdown and select 'Active'
steps.expectedResult: The list further narrows to show only Engineering employees with Active status

steps.action: Verify each visible employee row shows department 'Engineering' and status 'Active'
steps.expectedResult: Every row in the list shows Engineering as the department and Active as the status; no other combinations are present

expectedOutcome: Both filters work in conjunction, displaying only employees that match both the selected department and status simultaneously

title: [UI] employees-page: Filter combination that matches no employees shows an empty state or no-results message
preconditions: A department exists with only Active employees (e.g., all Sales employees are Active, none Inactive) OR a known combination that yields zero results

steps.action: Navigate to /employees
steps.expectedResult: The employees page loads with all employees listed

steps.action: Click the department filter dropdown and select a department that has no Inactive employees (e.g., 'Sales')
steps.expectedResult: The list shows only Sales employees, all with Active status
steps.testData: value: Sales (or whichever department has no Inactive members)

steps.action: Click the status filter dropdown and select 'Inactive'
steps.expectedResult: The list updates and no employee rows are displayed

steps.action: Observe the page for an empty state indicator
steps.expectedResult: A 'No employees found' message, empty state illustration, or equivalent indicator is displayed instead of an empty table with no feedback

expectedOutcome: When filters produce zero matching results, the UI clearly communicates that no employees match the current criteria

title: [UI] employee-drawer: Successfully add a new employee via the drawer form
preconditions: Application is running and accessible
No employee with the test data already exists in the system

steps.action: Navigate to the main employees page (e.g., /employees or /)
steps.expectedResult: The employees list page loads with an 'Add Employee' button visible

steps.action: Click the 'Add Employee' button
steps.expectedResult: The EmployeeDrawer slides open from the side, displaying the EmployeeForm with fields for first name, last name, email, phone, department, and job title

steps.action: Enter 'John' in the First Name field
steps.expectedResult: The First Name field displays 'John'

steps.action: Enter 'Doe' in the Last Name field
steps.expectedResult: The Last Name field displays 'Doe'

steps.action: Enter 'john.doe@example.com' in the Email field
steps.expectedResult: The Email field displays 'john.doe@example.com'

steps.action: Enter '555-123-4567' in the Phone field
steps.expectedResult: The Phone field displays '555-123-4567'

steps.action: Select 'Engineering' from the Department dropdown
steps.expectedResult: The Department dropdown shows 'Engineering' as the selected value

steps.action: Enter 'Software Engineer' in the Job Title field
steps.expectedResult: The Job Title field displays 'Software Engineer'

steps.action: Click the 'Save' or 'Submit' button inside the drawer
steps.expectedResult: The form submits, the drawer closes, a success toast/notification appears confirming the employee was added, and 'John Doe' appears in the employees list

expectedOutcome: A new employee 'John Doe' is successfully created and visible in the employee list, with a success notification displayed

title: [UI] employee-drawer: Submit form with all required fields empty shows validation errors
preconditions: Application is running and accessible

steps.action: Navigate to the employees page
steps.expectedResult: The employees list page loads

steps.action: Click the 'Add Employee' button
steps.expectedResult: The EmployeeDrawer opens with an empty form

steps.action: Without filling any fields, click the 'Save' button
steps.expectedResult: Validation error messages appear next to each required field (e.g., 'First name is required', 'Last name is required', 'Email is required'). The form is NOT submitted and the drawer remains open.

steps.action: Verify the drawer is still open and no success notification appeared
steps.expectedResult: The drawer remains visible with validation errors displayed. No success toast or notification is present.

expectedOutcome: Form submission is blocked, inline validation errors are shown for all required fields, and no employee is created

title: [UI] employee-drawer: Close drawer without saving discards form data
preconditions: Application is running and accessible

steps.action: Navigate to the employees page
steps.expectedResult: The employees list page loads

steps.action: Click the 'Add Employee' button
steps.expectedResult: The EmployeeDrawer opens

steps.action: Enter 'Temporary' in the First Name field and 'User' in the Last Name field
steps.expectedResult: Both fields display the entered values

steps.action: Click the close/cancel button (X icon or Cancel button) on the drawer
steps.expectedResult: The drawer closes and the employees list is displayed without a new entry for 'Temporary User'

steps.action: Click the 'Add Employee' button again
steps.expectedResult: The drawer reopens with all form fields empty/reset — no residual data from the previous session

expectedOutcome: Closing the drawer without saving discards all entered data, no employee is created, and reopening the drawer shows a fresh empty form

title: [UI] employee-form: Submit empty form shows all required field validation errors
preconditions: Application is running and accessible
No pre-filled employee data exists in the form

steps.action: Navigate to the route that contains the EmployeeForm component (e.g., / or /employees/new)
steps.expectedResult: The EmployeeForm is rendered with all input fields visible (e.g., First Name, Last Name, Email, Phone, Department, Job Title)

steps.action: Do not enter any data into any form field — leave all fields empty
steps.expectedResult: All form fields remain blank with no validation errors shown yet

steps.action: Click the Submit / Save button
steps.expectedResult: The form is NOT submitted. Validation error messages appear next to or below each required field

steps.action: Inspect the First Name field area for a validation message
steps.expectedResult: A visible error message such as 'First name is required' is displayed

steps.action: Inspect the Last Name field area for a validation message
steps.expectedResult: A visible error message such as 'Last name is required' is displayed

steps.action: Inspect the Email field area for a validation message
steps.expectedResult: A visible error message such as 'Email is required' is displayed

steps.action: Verify that the page has not navigated away or shown a success toast/banner
steps.expectedResult: The user remains on the same form page; no success notification is visible

expectedOutcome: All required field validation errors are displayed simultaneously when submitting a completely empty form, and the form submission is blocked

title: [UI] employee-form: Validation errors clear when user fills in the required field
preconditions: Application is running and accessible

steps.action: Navigate to the route containing the EmployeeForm
steps.expectedResult: The EmployeeForm is rendered with all fields empty

steps.action: Click the Submit / Save button without filling any fields
steps.expectedResult: Validation error messages appear for all required fields

steps.action: Enter a valid value in the First Name field
steps.expectedResult: The First Name field shows the entered value

steps.action: Observe the First Name validation error area (may trigger on blur or on input)
steps.expectedResult: The validation error for First Name disappears or clears after entering valid data and blurring/typing

steps.action: Verify that validation errors for the remaining unfilled required fields are still visible
steps.expectedResult: Error messages still appear next to Last Name, Email, and other required fields that remain empty

expectedOutcome: Validation errors are dynamically cleared for individual fields once valid data is provided, while other empty required fields retain their errors

title: [UI] confirm-dialog: Happy path – delete employee via confirmation dialog and verify removal
preconditions: At least one employee exists in the employee list
Application is running and accessible at the root route

steps.action: Navigate to the employee list page (e.g., /employees or /)
steps.expectedResult: The employee list renders with at least one employee row visible

steps.action: Note the name and count of employees currently displayed in the list
steps.expectedResult: Employee name and total count are recorded for later comparison

steps.action: Click the Delete button on the first employee row
steps.expectedResult: A confirmation dialog (ConfirmDialog) appears overlaying the page

steps.action: Verify the confirmation dialog displays a warning message asking if the user is sure they want to delete the employee
steps.expectedResult: Dialog contains text such as 'Are you sure' or 'confirm deletion' and shows the employee's name or a clear deletion context

steps.action: Verify the dialog has both a Confirm (e.g., 'Yes', 'Delete', 'Confirm') button and a Cancel button
steps.expectedResult: Both action buttons are visible and enabled inside the dialog

steps.action: Click the Confirm / Delete button inside the dialog
steps.expectedResult: The dialog closes, the employee is removed from the list, and the employee count decreases by one

steps.action: Verify a success toast or notification appears indicating the employee was deleted
steps.expectedResult: A success message such as 'Employee deleted' is displayed to the user

steps.action: Verify the previously noted employee name no longer appears in the employee list
steps.expectedResult: The deleted employee's row is absent from the list

expectedOutcome: Employee is successfully deleted after user confirms in the dialog, the list updates, and a success notification is shown

title: [UI] confirm-dialog: Cancel deletion – employee remains in the list after dismissing dialog
preconditions: At least one employee exists in the employee list
Application is running and accessible

steps.action: Navigate to the employee list page
steps.expectedResult: Employee list renders with at least one employee

steps.action: Note the name and total count of employees in the list
steps.expectedResult: Values recorded for comparison

steps.action: Click the Delete button on the first employee row
steps.expectedResult: The ConfirmDialog opens with a confirmation message

steps.action: Click the Cancel button inside the confirmation dialog
steps.expectedResult: The dialog closes and the user returns to the employee list view

steps.action: Verify the employee list still contains the same employees and count as before
steps.expectedResult: No employee was removed; the list is unchanged

steps.action: Verify no success or deletion toast/notification is shown
steps.expectedResult: No deletion-related notification is visible on the page

expectedOutcome: Cancelling the confirmation dialog leaves the employee list completely intact with no side effects

title: [UI] confirm-dialog: Dismiss dialog by clicking backdrop/overlay
preconditions: At least one employee exists in the employee list
Application is running

steps.action: Navigate to the employee list page
steps.expectedResult: Employee list is displayed

steps.action: Click the Delete button on any employee row
steps.expectedResult: ConfirmDialog opens with a backdrop/overlay behind it

steps.action: Click on the backdrop/overlay area outside the dialog content
steps.expectedResult: The dialog closes without performing the deletion

steps.action: Verify the employee list is unchanged
steps.expectedResult: All employees are still present; no deletion occurred

expectedOutcome: Clicking the backdrop dismisses the confirm dialog without deleting any employee

title: [UI] confirm-dialog: Dismiss dialog by pressing Escape key
preconditions: At least one employee exists in the employee list
Application is running

steps.action: Navigate to the employee list page
steps.expectedResult: Employee list is displayed

steps.action: Click the Delete button on any employee row
steps.expectedResult: ConfirmDialog appears

steps.action: Press the Escape key on the keyboard
steps.expectedResult: The dialog closes

steps.action: Verify the employee list remains unchanged and no deletion occurred
steps.expectedResult: All employees are still present in the list

expectedOutcome: Pressing Escape dismisses the dialog safely without deleting the employee

title: [UI] confirm-dialog: Dialog content displays correct employee context
preconditions: At least two employees exist with distinct names
Application is running

steps.action: Navigate to the employee list page
steps.expectedResult: Employee list displays at least two employees

steps.action: Note the name of the second employee in the list
steps.expectedResult: Employee name is recorded

steps.action: Click the Delete button on the second employee row
steps.expectedResult: ConfirmDialog opens

steps.action: Read the dialog body text
steps.expectedResult: The dialog text references the correct employee (e.g., contains the employee's name) so the user knows which employee will be deleted

steps.action: Click the Cancel button to close the dialog
steps.expectedResult: Dialog closes without side effects

expectedOutcome: The confirm dialog accurately identifies the employee targeted for deletion

title: [UI] confirm-dialog: Dialog blocks interaction with background content
preconditions: At least two employees exist in the list
Application is running

steps.action: Navigate to the employee list page
steps.expectedResult: Employee list is visible

steps.action: Click the Delete button on the first employee row
steps.expectedResult: ConfirmDialog appears with an overlay

steps.action: Attempt to click the Delete button on the second employee row (behind the dialog)
steps.expectedResult: The click does not register; no second dialog opens and no action is taken on the second employee

steps.action: Attempt to click any navigation links or other interactive elements behind the overlay
steps.expectedResult: No background interaction is possible; the dialog retains focus

steps.action: Click Cancel in the dialog to close it
steps.expectedResult: Dialog closes and background elements become interactive again

expectedOutcome: The ConfirmDialog is modal and prevents any interaction with the background until dismissed

title: [UI] confirm-dialog: Double-click on confirm does not trigger duplicate deletion
preconditions: At least two employees exist in the list
Application is running

steps.action: Navigate to the employee list page
steps.expectedResult: Employee list is displayed with at least two employees

steps.action: Note the total employee count
steps.expectedResult: Count is recorded

steps.action: Click the Delete button on the first employee
steps.expectedResult: ConfirmDialog opens

steps.action: Rapidly double-click the Confirm / Delete button inside the dialog
steps.expectedResult: The dialog closes and only one employee is removed from the list

steps.action: Verify the employee count decreased by exactly one
steps.expectedResult: Only the targeted employee was deleted; no additional employees were affected

steps.action: Verify no error messages or duplicate toast notifications are shown
steps.expectedResult: A single success notification is displayed (or no error)

expectedOutcome: Double-clicking confirm results in exactly one deletion with no errors or duplicate operations

title: [UI] confirm-dialog: Delete last remaining employee shows empty state
preconditions: Exactly one employee exists in the list
Application is running

steps.action: Navigate to the employee list page
steps.expectedResult: Employee list displays exactly one employee

steps.action: Click the Delete button on the only employee row
steps.expectedResult: ConfirmDialog opens

steps.action: Click the Confirm button inside the dialog
steps.expectedResult: The dialog closes and the employee is removed

steps.action: Verify the employee list now shows an empty state (e.g., 'No employees found' message or empty table)
steps.expectedResult: An appropriate empty state message or visual is displayed instead of an empty/broken table

expectedOutcome: Deleting the last employee results in a clean empty state in the UI

title: [UI] confirm-dialog: Deletion failure displays error message and keeps employee in list
preconditions: At least one employee exists in the list
Network conditions are simulated to cause API failure (e.g., via browser DevTools throttling or service worker mock)
Application is running

steps.action: Navigate to the employee list page
steps.expectedResult: Employee list is displayed

steps.action: Note the employee name and count
steps.expectedResult: Values recorded

steps.action: Simulate network failure (e.g., go offline in DevTools or intercept DELETE /api/employees/:id to return an error)
steps.expectedResult: Network is configured to fail the next DELETE request
steps.testData: Network: offline or mock 500 for DELETE /api/employees/:id

steps.action: Click the Delete button on the first employee
steps.expectedResult: ConfirmDialog opens

steps.action: Click the Confirm button
steps.expectedResult: The dialog processes and then an error notification or error message is displayed to the user

steps.action: Verify the employee is still present in the list
steps.expectedResult: The employee was not removed; the list count is unchanged

steps.action: Restore network connectivity
steps.expectedResult: Network is back online

expectedOutcome: When the deletion API fails, the user sees an error message and the employee remains in the list

title: [UI] confirm-dialog: Confirm button shows loading state during deletion
preconditions: At least one employee exists in the list
Application is running
Network can be throttled to slow 3G to observe loading state

steps.action: Navigate to the employee list page
steps.expectedResult: Employee list is displayed

steps.action: Throttle network to Slow 3G via browser DevTools
steps.expectedResult: Network is throttled to make API calls visibly slow

steps.action: Click the Delete button on any employee row
steps.expectedResult: ConfirmDialog opens

steps.action: Click the Confirm button and immediately observe the button state
steps.expectedResult: The Confirm button shows a loading indicator (spinner or 'Deleting…' text) and becomes disabled to prevent multiple clicks

steps.action: Wait for the deletion to complete
steps.expectedResult: The dialog closes, the employee is removed, and a success notification appears

steps.action: Reset network throttling to normal
steps.expectedResult: Network speed is restored

expectedOutcome: The confirm button provides visual loading feedback during the deletion API call and prevents duplicate submissions

title: [UI] employee-table: Empty state displayed when no employees exist
preconditions: Database contains zero employee records
Application is running and accessible

steps.action: Navigate to the employee table page (e.g., /employees or the route that renders EmployeeTable)
steps.expectedResult: The page loads without errors and the EmployeeTable component is rendered

steps.action: Observe the table body area where employee rows would normally appear
steps.expectedResult: An empty state message is displayed (e.g., 'No employees found' or similar placeholder text) instead of table rows
steps.testData: selector: [data-testid=empty-state] or visible text containing 'No employees' or 'No results'

steps.action: Verify that the table header row is still visible even though there are no data rows
steps.expectedResult: Column headers (e.g., Name, Email, Department, etc.) are visible in the table header

steps.action: Observe the pagination controls area below the table
steps.expectedResult: Pagination controls are either hidden or all page navigation buttons are disabled since there is no data to paginate

expectedOutcome: When no employees exist, the table displays an empty state message, column headers remain visible, and pagination controls are disabled or hidden

title: [UI] employee-table: Pagination navigates to page 2 and displays correct data
preconditions: Database contains enough employee records to span at least 2 pages (e.g., more than the default page size)
Application is running and accessible

steps.action: Navigate to the employee table page
steps.expectedResult: The page loads and the EmployeeTable displays the first page of employee records

steps.action: Count the number of employee rows displayed on page 1
steps.expectedResult: The number of rows matches the default page size (e.g., 10 or 20 rows)

steps.action: Note the name or identifier of the first employee in the current page
steps.expectedResult: First employee's data is visible and noted for later comparison

steps.action: Click the 'Next' page button or the page '2' button in the pagination controls
steps.expectedResult: The table updates and shows a different set of employee records (page 2 data)

steps.action: Verify the URL or active page indicator reflects page 2
steps.expectedResult: The page indicator highlights '2' as the current page, or the URL contains page=2
steps.testData: URL contains ?page=2 or active page button shows '2'

steps.action: Verify that the first employee row on page 2 is different from the first employee row on page 1
steps.expectedResult: The data displayed is a different set of employees, confirming pagination fetched new data
steps.testData: Compare noted employee from step 3 with first row on page 2

expectedOutcome: Clicking the pagination control to go to page 2 loads and displays the second page of employee records with the correct active page indicator

title: [UI] employee-table: Pagination 'Previous' button is disabled on first page
preconditions: Database contains enough employee records to span at least 2 pages
Application is running and accessible

steps.action: Navigate to the employee table page
steps.expectedResult: The EmployeeTable loads and displays the first page of results

steps.action: Locate the 'Previous' page button in the pagination controls
steps.expectedResult: The 'Previous' button is visible in the pagination area

steps.action: Check the disabled state of the 'Previous' button
steps.expectedResult: The 'Previous' button is disabled (grayed out, not clickable, or has disabled attribute)

steps.action: Attempt to click the disabled 'Previous' button
steps.expectedResult: Nothing happens — the page remains on page 1 and no navigation occurs

expectedOutcome: On the first page, the 'Previous' pagination button is disabled and clicking it has no effect

title: [UI] employee-table: Pagination 'Next' button is disabled on last page
preconditions: Database contains a known number of employee records that spans exactly N pages
Application is running and accessible

steps.action: Navigate to the employee table page
steps.expectedResult: The EmployeeTable loads and displays the first page of results

steps.action: Click the last page number button in the pagination controls (or repeatedly click 'Next' until reaching the final page)
steps.expectedResult: The table displays the last page of employee records
steps.testData: selector: last page number button in pagination

steps.action: Locate the 'Next' page button in the pagination controls
steps.expectedResult: The 'Next' button is visible

steps.action: Check the disabled state of the 'Next' button
steps.expectedResult: The 'Next' button is disabled (grayed out, not clickable, or has disabled attribute)

steps.action: Verify the number of rows on the last page is less than or equal to the page size
steps.expectedResult: The row count is between 1 and the default page size, confirming this is the final page

expectedOutcome: On the last page of results, the 'Next' pagination button is disabled and the table shows the remaining employee records

title: [UI] employee-table: Navigate forward then back returns to original page 1 data
preconditions: Database contains enough employee records to span at least 2 pages
Application is running and accessible

steps.action: Navigate to the employee table page
steps.expectedResult: The EmployeeTable loads and displays page 1 of results

steps.action: Note the content of the first and last employee rows displayed on page 1
steps.expectedResult: Page 1 employee data is recorded for comparison

steps.action: Click the 'Next' page button to navigate to page 2
steps.expectedResult: The table updates and displays page 2 employee records

steps.action: Verify the displayed data is different from page 1
steps.expectedResult: Different employees are shown, confirming page 2 loaded
steps.testData: Compare with noted data from step 2

steps.action: Click the 'Previous' page button to navigate back to page 1
steps.expectedResult: The table updates and shows the original page 1 data

steps.action: Compare the current first and last employee rows with the data noted in step 2
steps.expectedResult: The employee data matches exactly what was displayed on the initial page 1 load

expectedOutcome: Navigating to page 2 and then back to page 1 displays the same data that was originally on page 1, proving pagination round-trip works correctly

title: [UI] employee-table: Empty state after filtering yields no results
preconditions: Database contains employee records
Application is running and accessible
The EmployeeTable supports search or filter functionality

steps.action: Navigate to the employee table page
steps.expectedResult: The EmployeeTable loads and displays employee records

steps.action: Locate the search or filter input field above or near the table
steps.expectedResult: A search/filter input is visible and interactive

steps.action: Type a nonsensical string that will match no employee records into the search/filter field
steps.expectedResult: The input accepts the text and the table begins filtering

steps.action: Wait for the table to update after the filter is applied
steps.expectedResult: The table shows the empty state message (e.g., 'No employees found' or 'No results') instead of employee rows
steps.testData: selector: [data-testid=empty-state] or visible text 'No employees' or 'No results'

steps.action: Verify that pagination controls are disabled or hidden since there are no results
steps.expectedResult: Pagination buttons are either not rendered or all disabled

steps.action: Clear the search/filter input field
steps.expectedResult: The table reloads and the original employee records reappear with pagination active
steps.testData: Clear the input and verify employee rows return

expectedOutcome: Filtering with a non-matching query displays the empty state, and clearing the filter restores the original employee list

title: [UI] employee-table: Active page number is visually highlighted in pagination
preconditions: Database contains enough employee records to span at least 3 pages
Application is running and accessible

steps.action: Navigate to the employee table page
steps.expectedResult: The EmployeeTable loads and page 1 is displayed

steps.action: Observe the pagination controls and identify which page number is visually highlighted or styled as active
steps.expectedResult: Page '1' button has an active/selected visual style (e.g., different background color, bold text, or aria-current='page')
steps.testData: selector: pagination button with active class or aria-current=page

steps.action: Click the page '2' button in the pagination controls
steps.expectedResult: The table updates to show page 2 data and the page '2' button now has the active visual style

steps.action: Verify that page '1' button is no longer highlighted as active
steps.expectedResult: Page '1' button returns to the default (non-active) style
steps.testData: selector: button containing text '1' — should NOT have active class

steps.action: Click the page '3' button in the pagination controls
steps.expectedResult: The table updates to show page 3 data and the page '3' button now has the active visual style while '2' returns to default

expectedOutcome: The currently active page number is always visually distinguished in the pagination controls, and it updates correctly when navigating between pages

title: [UI] employee-table: Single page of results hides or disables all pagination navigation
preconditions: Database contains a small number of employee records that fit within a single page (e.g., fewer than the page size)
Application is running and accessible

steps.action: Navigate to the employee table page
steps.expectedResult: The EmployeeTable loads and displays all employee records on a single page

steps.action: Verify that all employee records are visible in the table
steps.expectedResult: The row count matches the total number of employees in the database

steps.action: Check the pagination controls area
steps.expectedResult: Pagination controls are either not rendered at all, or both 'Previous' and 'Next' buttons are disabled, and only page '1' is shown

steps.action: If pagination is visible, attempt to click the 'Next' button
steps.expectedResult: The button does not respond (disabled) and the table remains on the same page with the same data

expectedOutcome: When all records fit on a single page, pagination navigation is either hidden or fully disabled to prevent unnecessary page changes

title: [UI] employee-table: Table columns and headers render correctly on page load
preconditions: Database contains at least one employee record
Application is running and accessible

steps.action: Navigate to the employee table page
steps.expectedResult: The page loads without errors and the EmployeeTable component is rendered

steps.action: Verify that the table element is present in the DOM and visible on screen
steps.expectedResult: A table element is rendered and visible

steps.action: Inspect the table header row for expected column names
steps.expectedResult: Column headers such as Name, Email, Department (or similar expected columns) are all displayed in the header row

steps.action: Verify at least one data row is rendered in the table body
steps.expectedResult: One or more employee rows are visible with data populated in each column

steps.action: Check that each cell in the first data row contains non-empty text
steps.expectedResult: Every cell in the first row displays content — no blank or missing cells

expectedOutcome: The employee table renders with correct column headers and populated data rows on initial page load

title: [UI] employee-table: Loading state appears before data is rendered
preconditions: Database contains employee records
Application is running and accessible
Network can be throttled or observed to see loading state

steps.action: Throttle the network to slow 3G (or intercept the API request with a delay) to make the loading state visible
steps.expectedResult: Network is throttled and API responses will be delayed
steps.testData: DevTools > Network > Slow 3G or intercept GET /api/employees

steps.action: Navigate to the employee table page
steps.expectedResult: The page begins loading and a loading indicator (spinner, skeleton, or 'Loading...' text) is displayed in the table area
steps.testData: route: /employees; selector: [data-testid=loading] or spinner or skeleton

steps.action: Wait for the API response to complete
steps.expectedResult: The loading indicator disappears and employee data rows are rendered in the table

steps.action: Remove the network throttle and click the 'Next' page button
steps.expectedResult: A brief loading state may appear during pagination transition before the next page of data renders

expectedOutcome: A loading indicator is shown while employee data is being fetched, and it transitions to the data view once the response is received
